mappings.rationaleAI: Hierarchical stratification by economic position encompassing differentiation and stratification concepts (A), class-caste relations (B.1), divergent theories of class (B.2), characteristics of upper, working, and middle classes (B.3), social mobility (B.4), and the idea of a classless society (B.5).
Societal condition of equal rights and status, relevant to the effects of differentiation on the individual (A.3.c) and the idea of a classless society (B.5).
Capacity to influence others' actions through coercion, institutional structure, and cultural hegemony, directly constituting power as a form of stratification (A.3.b).
Unjustified distinctions based on group membership, constituting a factor producing social and cultural differences (A.2).
Social dimensions of gender identity relevant to sex differentiation as a factor producing social differences (A.2).
Beliefs about racial superiority relevant to racial differentiation as a factor producing social differences (A.2).
Political domination creating racial and cultural stratification (A.2), with colonial caste-like hierarchies relevant to class-caste relations (B.1).
Class struggle theory addressing economic basis of stratification (A.3.a), divergent conceptions of class importance (B.2), and the vision of a classless communist society (B.5).
Productive activity embedded in class structures with division of labor, relevant to economic differentiation as the basis of stratification (A.3.a).
Legally enforceable rights over tangible and intangible things, mediating economic differentiation as the basis of stratification (A.3.a).
Educational access as a factor producing intellectual differentiation (A.2) and a vehicle for social mobility (B.4).
Ideology advocating abolition of class distinctions, directly representing the idea of a classless society (B.5).
Political philosophy centering individual rights and equality before the law, relevant to social mobility (B.4) and approximations to equality of conditions (B.5).
Scientific study of society with stratification as a major subfield, encompassing differentiation theory (A) and varieties of stratification (B).
Transition to factory manufacturing reshaping economic differentiation (A.3.a) and creating modern working and middle class characteristics (B.3).
Universal human practice with social functions of status signaling, wealth display, and gender differentiation, relevant to social and cultural factors producing differences (A.2).
Condition of lacking resources representing the lower end of economic differentiation (A.3.a), with characteristics of lower-class position (B.3).
Universal moral principles of equality and non-discrimination relevant to the idea of a classless society and equality of conditions (B.5).
Forced transportation creating racial stratification (A.2), with plantation slavery as a caste-like system (B.1).
Extension of power creating social and cultural differences through racial and economic differentiation (A.2).
Conditions limiting participation as a factor in social differentiation (A.2) with effects on the individual's relation to society (A.3.c).
Prejudice based on sex producing social differentiation through wage gaps, hiring bias, and institutional barriers (A.2).
Ability to read and write correlating with academic achievement and economic mobility, relevant to intellectual differentiation (A.2) and social mobility (B.4).
Intellectual movement emphasizing individual liberty and equality, philosophically grounding approximations to equality of conditions (B.5).
Process of colonial territories achieving independence, dismantling racial stratification hierarchies (A.2) and enabling social mobility for formerly colonized populations (B.4).
